disclaimer: This question bank is curated for Gemini/Google interview preparation based on common technical interview patterns. The 'Answer' represents the optimal algorithmic approach. 'Relevant Options' are plausible distractors similar to MCQ format. Questions cover arrays, DP, graphs, strings, and system design fundamentals.

questions.title: Two Sum
questions.question: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target. You may assume exactly one solution.
questions.answer: Use a hash map to store complements while iterating O(n) time and O(n) space.
relevant_options: Nested loops for all pairs O(n²)
Sort and use two pointers O(n log n)
Use a hash map to store complements O(n)
Use a stack to track elements

questions.title: Valid Parentheses
questions.question: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. Open brackets must be closed in the correct order.
questions.answer: Use a stack: push opening brackets, pop and match when closing brackets are encountered.
relevant_options: Count number of each bracket type and compare
Use a stack with mapping dictionary
Replace valid pairs recursively until empty
Check if string length is even then compare halves

questions.title: Merge Intervals
questions.question: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals.
questions.answer: Sort by start time, then merge if current start <= last end; otherwise add new interval.
relevant_options: Sort by end time and merge backwards
Sort by start and use stack for merging

questions.title: 3Sum
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j != k and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. The solution must not contain duplicate triplets.
questions.answer: Sort array, fix first element, use two pointers for remaining to find pairs summing to -first.
relevant_options: Triple nested loops with hash set for deduplication
Sort and use two pointers with skip duplicates
Use hash map for pair sums

questions.title: Container With Most Water
questions.question: Given n non-negative integers representing heights, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that holds the most water.
questions.answer: Two pointers from both ends, move the shorter pointer inward, tracking max area.
relevant_options: Two pointers moving both inward simultaneously
Two pointers moving shorter inward, compute area each step
Use monotonic stack to find boundaries

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.question: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
questions.answer: Sliding window with two pointers and a hash map storing last index of each character.
relevant_options: Generate all substrings and check O(n³)
Sliding window with set and left pointer
Dynamic programming with last occurrence array

questions.title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's Algorithm)
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum.
questions.answer: Kadane's algorithm: current sum = max(nums[i], current + nums[i]), track global max.
relevant_options: Prefix sums with brute force O(n²)
Divide and conquer O(n log n)

questions.title: Product of Array Except Self
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i]. Must run in O(n) without division.
questions.answer: Compute prefix products and suffix products separately, then multiply them for each index.
relevant_options: Divide total product by each element (fails for zeros)
Use two passes: left to right then right to left

questions.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
questions.question: Given a rotated sorted array (no duplicates) and a target, search for target in O(log n) time.
questions.answer: Modified binary search: determine which half is sorted by comparing mid with left/right.
relevant_options: Find pivot then binary search on both halves
Modified binary with sorted check on each half
Use ternary search (doesn't work for rotation)

questions.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
questions.question: Given a rotated sorted array (no duplicates), find the minimum element in O(log n) time.
questions.answer: Binary search: if mid > right, min is in right half; otherwise min is in left half.
relevant_options: Binary search comparing mid with right

questions.title: Combination Sum
questions.question: Given an array of distinct integers and a target, find all unique combinations where numbers sum to target. Same number may be used unlimited times.
questions.answer: Backtracking with index to avoid duplicates, subtract target on each recursive call.
relevant_options: DFS with remain target and start index
Greedy with largest first then backtrack

questions.title: Permutations
questions.question: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all possible permutations.
questions.answer: Backtracking with used boolean array, add to result when size equals nums length.
relevant_options: Use next_permutation repeatedly on sorted array

questions.title: Subsets
questions.question: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set).
questions.answer: Backtracking adding/removing elements, or bitmask iteration from 0 to 2^n-1.

questions.title: Generate Parentheses
questions.question: Given n pairs of parentheses, generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
questions.answer: Backtracking: add '(' if left < n, add ')' if right < left, building result string.
relevant_options: DP building from smaller n combinations

questions.title: Word Search
questions.question: Given an m x n grid of characters and a word, return true if the word exists. Letters must be adjacent horizontally or vertically, same cell cannot be used twice.
questions.answer: DFS backtracking from each starting cell, marking visited temporarily.
relevant_options: DFS with visited set and pruning

questions.title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
questions.question: Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent (phone keypad mapping).
questions.answer: Backtracking building string, mapping digits to corresponding letter arrays.
relevant_options: BFS queue building combinations level by level

questions.title: Rotate Image
questions.question: Rotate an n x n matrix 90 degrees clockwise in-place.
questions.answer: Transpose the matrix, then reverse each row.
relevant_options: Create new matrix and copy (O(n²) space)

questions.title: Spiral Matrix
questions.question: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements in spiral order.
questions.answer: Track boundaries (top, bottom, left, right), traverse perimeter, shrink boundaries each iteration.
relevant_options: Simulate with direction changes using matrix
BFS with visited set and direction priority

questions.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
questions.question: Given an m x n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in-place using O(1) extra space.
questions.answer: Use first row and first column as markers; track if first row/col need zeroing separately.
relevant_options: Use two sets to track rows and cols to zero O(m+n)
Create copy of matrix and check O(mn)
Mark with special sentinel value then convert
Use boolean flags for rows and cols

questions.title: Group Anagrams
questions.question: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together.
questions.answer: Sort each string to use as key in hash map, values are lists of original strings.
relevant_options: Character count tuple as key (26-length array)
Sort each word and use map of sorted->list

questions.title: Top K Frequent Elements
questions.question: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements.
questions.answer: Count frequencies, use min-heap of size k or bucket sort by frequency.
relevant_options: Sort by frequency after counting O(n log n)
Use priority queue with custom comparator
Bucket sort with array of lists

questions.title: Encode and Decode TinyURL
questions.question: Design a URL shortening service. encode(longUrl) returns shortUrl, decode(shortUrl) returns original.
questions.answer: Use counter or hash to generate unique ID, store in hash map, convert ID to base62 string.
relevant_options: Use MD5 hash and take first 6 chars
Increment counter and use base62 encoding
Use random UUID as key in database

questions.title: Design a Leaderboard
questions.question: Design a leaderboard with addScore(playerId, score), top(K) returns sum of top K scores, reset(playerId) resets score to 0.
questions.answer: Use hash map for player scores, and TreeMap for score frequencies or min-heap for top K.
relevant_options: Use array of size N for player scores, sort for top K
Use priority queue with lazy updates
Use balanced BST (TreeMap) for score ranking
Use bucket sort if scores are bounded

questions.title: Design Circular Queue
questions.question: Design a circular queue using a fixed-size array. Implement enQueue, deQueue, Front, Rear, isEmpty, isFull.
questions.answer: Use head and tail pointers with modulo arithmetic; maintain count or use one empty slot to distinguish full/empty.
relevant_options: Use two stacks to simulate queue
Use linked list with head and tail
Use array with head/tail and count variable
Use two pointers with modulo and size tracking

questions.title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
questions.question: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
questions.answer: Trie node contains children array (size 26) and boolean isEnd; insert traverses/creates nodes.
relevant_options: Use hash map for children instead of array
Use linked list of nodes per level
Store all words and do prefix check O(n*m)
Use binary search tree of strings

questions.title: Add and Search Word - Data structure design
questions.question: Design a data structure that supports adding words and searching for words where '.' can match any single character.
questions.answer: Use trie with DFS search for '.' wildcard; traverse all children when wildcard encountered.
relevant_options: Use hash set of all words and check pattern with regex
Use trie with BFS for dot matching
Store words by length and binary search

questions.title: LRU Cache
questions.question: Design an LRU (Least Recently Used) cache with get and put operations in O(1) average time.
questions.answer: Use hash map (key -> node) and doubly linked list to maintain order; move accessed nodes to head.
relevant_options: Use LinkedHashMap in Java or OrderedDict in Python
Use queue with timestamp and map
Use two stacks for recent and old
Use array with timestamps and sweep

questions.title: Min Stack
questions.question: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
questions.answer: Use two stacks: main stack and min stack storing current minimum at each level.
relevant_options: Store (value, current min) as pair in single stack
Calculate min difference and store delta
Use min variable and re-scan on pop (O(n))
Use priority queue as secondary structure

questions.title: Maximum Width of Binary Tree
questions.question: Given the root of a binary tree, return the maximum width of the tree among all levels. Width is the length between leftmost and rightmost non-null nodes, with nulls between counted.
questions.answer: BFS with indexing: root index 0, left child = 2*index, right = 2*index+1; width = last - first + 1 per level.
relevant_options: DFS with position tracking using hash map
Use level order traversal with null placeholder

questions.title: Binary Tree Right Side View
questions.question: Given the root of a binary tree, return the values of the nodes you can see when standing on the right side (ordered from top to bottom).
questions.answer: BFS level order and take last node of each level, or DFS with level tracking and first right node.
relevant_options: Level order traversal with queue, add rightmost node each level
DFS right-first preorder, add when level not seen
BFS and store entire level then take last

questions.title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
questions.question: Given the root of a binary search tree and an integer k, return the kth smallest value (1-indexed).
questions.answer: In-order traversal (left-root-right) yields sorted order; return kth element visited.
relevant_options: Augment tree with size of left subtree

questions.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
questions.question: Given a binary tree, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes.
questions.answer: Recursively search left and right; if both sides return non-null, current is LCA.
relevant_options: Store parent pointers and find intersection
Find paths from root to both nodes and compare

questions.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
questions.question: Given a binary tree, find the maximum path sum where the path can start and end at any node. The path must go parent-child (cannot skip levels).
questions.answer: DFS returns max gain from node to leaf; track global max = max(global, node.val + left_gain + right_gain).
relevant_options: Brute force check all paths O(n²)
Post-order traversal with negative branch pruning
Rooted tree DP with two options (include/not include node)

questions.title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
questions.question: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree. The tree can be any shape (not necessarily BST).
questions.answer: Use level order BFS with null markers, or preorder with sentinel for null children.
relevant_options: Preorder traversal with delimiters and null markers
Level order with queue storing null

questions.title: Validate Binary Search Tree
questions.question: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST (left node values < root, right node values > root, recursively).
questions.answer: In-order traversal and check if values are strictly increasing; or DFS with valid range (min, max).
relevant_options: Recursive with lower and upper bound

questions.title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node
questions.question: Given a perfect binary tree, connect each node to its next right node. Use O(1) extra space.
questions.answer: Use next pointers of current level to traverse next level without queue.
relevant_options: BFS with queue storing nodes level by level
Recursive using left->right and right->next of parent
Iterative using dummy head for each level

questions.title: Number of Islands
questions.question: Given an m x n 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands. Island is surrounded by water and formed by adjacent (horizontal/vertical) land cells.
questions.answer: DFS/BFS on each unvisited '1', marking visited, increment count for each exploration.
relevant_options: Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) on land cells
BFS with queue for flood fill
DFS recursion (may cause stack overflow for large grids)
Use visited matrix or mark grid in-place

questions.title: Course Schedule
questions.question: There are n courses labeled 0 to n-1. Prerequisites given as pairs [a,b] meaning b needed before a. Determine if all courses can be finished.
questions.answer: Detect cycle in directed graph using DFS with states (unvisited, visiting, visited) or Kahn's topological sort.
relevant_options: Union Find (not suitable for directed cycles)

questions.title: Course Schedule II
questions.question: Given courses and prerequisites, return the ordering of courses to finish all. If impossible, return empty array.
questions.answer: Kahn's BFS topological sort: enqueue nodes with indegree 0, remove edges, add to result.
relevant_options: DFS post-order with stack for ordering
DFS with color states and result list

questions.title: Clone Graph
questions.question: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph.
questions.answer: BFS or DFS using hash map to map original nodes to cloned nodes, clone neighbors recursively.
relevant_options: Create new nodes and copy adjacency separately

questions.title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
questions.question: Given an m x n matrix of heights, water can flow to adjacent cells with equal or lower height. Find cells where water can flow to both Pacific (top/left edges) and Atlantic (bottom/right edges).
questions.answer: DFS/BFS from Pacific edge and Atlantic edge separately, intersect reachable cells.
relevant_options: DFS from each cell to both oceans (O((mn)²))
Union Find on cells with flow direction
DP with memoization on flow reachability

questions.title: Graph Valid Tree
questions.question: Given n nodes labeled 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges, determine if the graph forms a valid tree (connected and acyclic).
questions.answer: Check if edges.length == n-1 and graph is connected (DFS/BFS).
relevant_options: Union Find: if all edges unite nodes without cycle and total edges = n-1
DFS with visited set and parent tracking
BFS to check connectivity and cycle
Check if any node has degree > 2 for tree property?

questions.title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
questions.question: Given n nodes labeled 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges, find the number of connected components.
questions.answer: Union Find or DFS/BFS to count number of disjoint sets.
relevant_options: Adjacency list then DFS traversal count

questions.title: Minimum Height Trees
questions.question: Given a tree of n nodes and edges, find all minimum height trees' root labels. A minimum height tree has the smallest possible height among all possible roots.
questions.answer: Trim leaves level by level (topological sort) until 1 or 2 nodes remain.
relevant_options: Calculate height for each node using DFS O(n²)
BFS from each leaf with degree tracking
Find diameter of tree then middle nodes

questions.title: Alien Dictionary
questions.question: Given a sorted dictionary of an alien language (list of words), find the order of characters (if exists).
questions.answer: Compare adjacent words to find character ordering, build graph, topological sort.
relevant_options: BFS Kahn's topological sort on character graph
DFS with cycle detection and post-order
Compare all pairs of words O(n² * L)

questions.title: Word Ladder
questions.question: Given beginWord, endWord, and a word list, find the length of shortest transformation sequence where each step changes one letter and each intermediate word must be in word list.
questions.answer: BFS from beginWord, generating all possible next words by changing each position to 'a'-'z'.

questions.title: Minimum Knight Moves
questions.question: On an infinite chessboard, find the minimum number of moves for a knight to reach (x, y) from (0, 0). Coordinates can be positive or negative.
questions.answer: BFS with symmetry (use absolute values) and pruning; or mathematical formula with DP.
relevant_options: BFS with visited set and queue

questions.title: Snakes and Ladders
questions.question: On an N x N board with snakes and ladders, find the minimum number of dice rolls (1-6) to reach the last square.
questions.answer: BFS from square 1 to N²; treat snake/ladder as immediate teleportation.
relevant_options: BFS with visited set on squares

questions.title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
questions.question: Given flight prices and at most K stops (K+1 edges), find cheapest price from src to dst. Return -1 if not reachable.
questions.answer: Bellman-Ford variant (DP on stops) or BFS with priority queue (Dijkstra with stop limit).
relevant_options: Modified Dijkstra with stop count in state
BFS with queue for each stop level
DFS with pruning on stops and cost

questions.title: Network Delay Time
questions.question: Given network of n nodes, travel times as directed edges, and a source node k, find the minimum time for all nodes to receive a signal. Return -1 if some node unreachable.
questions.answer: Dijkstra's algorithm from source, answer = max distance to all nodes.

questions.title: Reconstruct Itinerary
questions.question: Given a list of airline tickets (from, to), reconstruct the itinerary in lexical order starting from 'JFK'. Use each ticket exactly once.
questions.answer: Hierholzer's algorithm for Eulerian path: DFS with multiset, process destinations in lexical reverse order.
relevant_options: DFS with visited set on edges
Greedy pick smallest each step (may fail)
Use adjacency list with priority queue

questions.title: Evaluate Division
questions.question: Given equations like a/b = 2.0 and queries like a/c, evaluate each query. Return -1.0 if unknown.
questions.answer: Build graph with nodes as variables, edge weights as quotient; DFS or Floyd-Warshall for all pairs.
relevant_options: Union Find with weight (ratio to root)
DFS on each query with visited set

questions.title: Possible Bipartition
questions.question: Given n people and dislikes pairs where two people cannot be in the same group, determine if it's possible to split into two groups.
questions.answer: Check if graph is bipartite using BFS/DFS with 2-coloring.
relevant_options: Union Find with parity tracking (bipartite DSU)

questions.title: Is Graph Bipartite?
questions.question: Given an undirected graph (adjacency list), determine if it is bipartite (2-colorable).
questions.answer: BFS/DFS with alternating colors; conflict means not bipartite.

questions.title: Climbing Stairs
questions.question: You are climbing a staircase with n steps. Each time you can climb 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways to reach the top?
questions.answer: Fibonacci-like DP: dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2] with base cases dp[1]=1, dp[2]=2.
relevant_options: Dynamic programming with recursion and memoization

questions.title: House Robber
questions.question: Given integer array nums representing money in each house, cannot rob adjacent houses. Return maximum amount you can rob.
questions.answer: DP: rob[i] = max(rob[i-1], rob[i-2] + nums[i]), space-optimized to two variables.
relevant_options: Use two variables prev2, prev1, current

questions.title: House Robber II (Circular)
questions.question: Houses are arranged in a circle (first and last adjacent). Cannot rob adjacent houses. Return max amount.
questions.answer: Two cases: rob houses 0 to n-2, and 1 to n-1; take max of both.
relevant_options: DP with state tracking first house picked
Break the circle by considering two linear arrays
DP with three states (first not robbed, could be robbed)

questions.title: Maximum Product Subarray
questions.question: Given integer array nums (may contain negatives), find contiguous subarray with largest product.
questions.answer: Track both max and min product so far (because negative times negative becomes positive).
relevant_options: Kadane's algorithm for sum (doesn't work for product)
Divide and conquer O(n log n)

questions.title: Coin Change
questions.question: Given coin denominations and an amount, return the fewest coins needed to make that amount. Return -1 if impossible.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i] = min(dp[i-coin] + 1) for each coin, initialize with large value, dp[0]=0.

questions.title: Coin Change II (Number of combinations)
questions.question: Given coins and an amount, return the number of combinations that make up that amount (unlimited coins, order doesn't matter).
questions.answer: DP outer loop over coins and inner loop over amounts to avoid permutations.
relevant_options: DP outer loop over amounts (counts permutations)

questions.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence (not necessarily contiguous).
questions.answer: Patience sorting: maintain tails array, binary search for each num to replace or append.
relevant_options: DP O(n²): LIS ending at each index
Binary search on tails O(n log n)

questions.title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
questions.question: Given an integer array nums, return true if the array can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.
questions.answer: If total sum odd, return false. Use DP (knapsack) to check if subset sum = total/2 can be achieved.

questions.title: Decode Ways
questions.question: Given a digit string, count number of ways to decode it to letters (A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26).
relevant_options: Iterative with two variables space optimization

questions.title: Unique Paths
questions.question: Robot moves only right and down from top-left to bottom-right of m x n grid. Count number of unique paths.
questions.answer: Combinatorics: C(m+n-2, m-1) or DP grid with dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1].

questions.title: Unique Paths II (with Obstacles)
questions.question: Same as Unique Paths but with obstacles (marked 1). Robot cannot pass through obstacles.
questions.answer: DP, set dp[i][j]=0 if obstacle; otherwise dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j]+dp[i][j-1] (with boundary checks).

questions.title: Minimum Path Sum
questions.question: Given a grid with non-negative numbers, find a path from top-left to bottom-right minimizing sum, moving only right or down.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i][j] = grid[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]) with boundary initialization.

questions.title: Jump Game
questions.question: You start at index 0, each element represents max jump length. Determine if you can reach the last index.
questions.answer: Greedy: maintain farthest reachable index; if at any point i > farthest, return false.

questions.title: Jump Game II (Minimum Jumps)
questions.question: Given array, find minimum number of jumps to reach the last index. It is guaranteed reachable.
questions.answer: Greedy BFS: track current_end, farthest, jumps++; when i reaches current_end, set current_end=farthest.
relevant_options: Single pass greedy with two pointers

questions.title: Maximum Length of Pair Chain
questions.question: You are given n pairs (a,b) where a < b. Form a chain such that b of previous < a of next. Find longest chain length.
questions.answer: Sort pairs by second element (end), greedy pick earliest finish to maximize chain.
relevant_options: Sort by first element and DP O(n²)
Sort by end and then by start

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
questions.question: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring.
questions.answer: Expand around center for each possible center (2n-1 centers: single char and between chars).

questions.title: Palindromic Substrings
questions.question: Count the number of palindromic substrings in a given string.
questions.answer: Expand around center and count palindromes for each center (odd and even length).

questions.title: Edit Distance
questions.question: Given two strings, find minimum number of operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert one to the other.
questions.answer: DP table: if chars match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]; else dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]).

questions.title: Longest Common Subsequence
questions.question: Given two strings, return the length of the longest common subsequence (not necessarily contiguous).
questions.answer: DP table: if match, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1] + 1; else dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]).

questions.title: Distinct Subsequences
questions.question: Given strings s and t, count the number of distinct subsequences of s that equal t.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i][j] = number of ways using first i chars of s to form first j chars of t.

questions.title: Regular Expression Matching
questions.question: Implement regular expression matching with '.' (any char) and '*' (zero or more preceding element). Support full string match.
questions.answer: DP with cases: if next char is '*', consider zero or more of current char; else single char match.

questions.title: Wildcard Matching
questions.question: Implement wildcard pattern matching with '?' (any char) and '*' (any sequence including empty).
questions.answer: DP or greedy: track last '*' positions to backtrack when mismatch occurs.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown
questions.question: You may complete as many transactions as you like, but after selling you must wait 1 day (cooldown) before buying again.
questions.answer: State machine DP: states are 'rest', 'buy', 'sell' with transitions between them.
relevant_options: Recursive with memoization on (index, holding, cooldown)

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock IV (K transactions)
questions.question: Given prices and maximum k transactions, find maximum profit (one share at a time).
questions.answer: If k >= n/2, treat as unlimited transactions; else DP with profit array and best balance.

questions.title: Maximum Sum Circular Subarray
questions.question: Given a circular integer array, find maximum sum of a non-empty subarray (subarray can wrap around).
questions.answer: Max of (max subarray using Kadane) or (total sum - min subarray) if not all negative.
relevant_options: Kadane for max subarray and min subarray
Concatenate array and sliding window of size n

questions.title: Burst Balloons
questions.question: Burst balloons each with coins = nums[left] * nums[i] * nums[right]; find maximum coins from bursting all.
questions.answer: DP on intervals with virtual boundary 1s; try last balloon to burst in subarray.
relevant_options: Recursive divide and conquer with memo

questions.title: Stone Game
questions.question: Two players pick from either end of stone pile; each wants to maximize sum. Return true if first player can win.
questions.answer: DP for optimal play; first player wins if total sum is odd or total can be split. Actually always true for even length?
relevant_options: Mathematical: first player always wins with even pile count

questions.title: Predict the Winner
questions.question: Given scores array, two players pick from left or right; determine if first player can win (score >= second player).
questions.answer: DP[i][j] = max score difference current player can achieve on subarray i..j compared to opponent.

questions.title: Minimum Falling Path Sum
questions.question: Given n x n matrix, find minimum sum path from top row to bottom row, moving to adjacent columns below (down, down-left, down-right).
questions.answer: DP row by row: dp[row][col] = grid[row][col] + min(prev[col-1], prev[col], prev[col+1]) with boundary checks.

questions.title: Triangle (Minimum Path Sum)
questions.question: Given a triangle array, find minimum path sum from top to bottom moving only to adjacent numbers below.
questions.answer: Bottom-up DP: start from second last row, update each element as min of two children + current.

questions.title: Maximum Square Area in Matrix
questions.question: Given binary matrix, find the maximum side length of a square containing all 1's.
questions.answer: DP: dp[i][j] = min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) + 1 if matrix[i][j]=='1'; track max.

questions.title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable
questions.question: Given a 2D matrix, implement sumRegion(row1, col1, row2, col2) returning sum of submatrix inclusive.
questions.answer: Precompute prefix sums: dp[i][j] = sum of rectangle from (0,0) to (i-1,j-1); query = dp[r2+1][c2+1] - dp[r1][c2+1] - dp[r2+1][c1] + dp[r1][c1].
relevant_options: Store matrix and compute on each query O(n)

questions.title: N-Queens

questions.title: Count Complete Tree Nodes
questions.question: Count nodes in a complete binary tree faster than O(n).
questions.answer: Compute left height and right height; if equal, nodes = 2^height -1; else 1 + count(left) + count(right).

questions.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
questions.question: Find LCA of two nodes in BST (values guaranteed to exist).
questions.answer: Use BST property: if both > root, go right; both < root go left; else root is LCA.

questions.title: Binary Tree Cameras
questions.question: Place cameras on nodes; each camera monitors itself, parent, children. Find minimum cameras to cover entire tree.
questions.answer: DFS post-order with states: 0=not covered, 1=covered but no camera, 2=has camera.

questions.title: House Robber III (Binary Tree)
questions.question: Rob houses arranged as binary tree; cannot rob directly connected nodes. Max sum.
questions.answer: Return [rob current, not rob current]; not rob = max(left rob, left not) + max(right rob, right not); rob = node.val + left not + right not.
relevant_options: BFS level then DP on tree

questions.title: All Nodes Distance K in Binary Tree
questions.question: Given binary tree, target node, and integer k, return all nodes at distance exactly k from target.
questions.answer: Build parent map via DFS; BFS from target outward k steps using visited set.
relevant_options: DFS to mark depth from target with parent
BFS on tree with parent pointers

questions.title: Verify Preorder Serialization of a Binary Tree
questions.question: Given a preorder serialization with null markers '#', check if valid binary tree.
questions.answer: Use degree property: slots = 1; for each node, slots--; if node not '#', slots+=2; if slots < 0 invalid; final slots == 0.
relevant_options: Stack simulation: push numbers, pop on '#'

questions.title: Find Bottom Left Tree Value
questions.question: Find the leftmost value in the last row of a binary tree.
questions.answer: BFS level order: first node of each level; return value of last level's first node.
relevant_options: DFS with depth tracking and leftmost
Reverse level order (right to left) then last
Queue BFS storing leftmost per level

questions.title: Sum Root to Leaf Numbers
questions.question: Each root-to-leaf path forms a number. Return sum of all such numbers.
questions.answer: DFS: pass current number (prev*10 + node.val); when leaf, add to sum.

questions.title: Continuous Subarray Sum
questions.question: Given array of non-negative integers and k, check if subarray of length at least 2 has sum divisible by k.
questions.answer: Use prefix sum modulo k: if same remainder seen before and distance >=2, return true.
relevant_options: Prefix sums with set for remainders

questions.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
questions.question: Count number of subarrays where sum equals k (can be negative).
questions.answer: Prefix sum with hash map: map[sum]++ ; ans += map[sum - k] at each step.
relevant_options: Prefix sums array then binary search (not for negatives)

questions.title: Find Pivot Index
questions.question: Find pivot index where sum of left equals sum of right (excluding pivot).
questions.answer: Total sum - left sum - nums[i] == left sum; so left sum * 2 == total - nums[i].
relevant_options: Compute left sum and total sum iterate

questions.title: Maximum Size Subarray Sum Equals k
questions.question: Find longest subarray length with sum = k (can be negative).
questions.answer: Prefix sum with map storing first occurrence of each sum; ans = max(ans, i - map[sum-k]).
relevant_options: Binary search on length with prefix sums

questions.title: Range Sum Query - Immutable
questions.question: Given integer array, implement sumRange(i,j) returning sum from i to j inclusive.
questions.answer: Precompute prefix sums; sumRange(i,j) = prefix[j+1] - prefix[i].
relevant_options: Store array and sum each query O(n)
DP with 2D table for all ranges

questions.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
questions.question: Find minimal length subarray with sum >= target (positive integers only).
questions.answer: Sliding window: expand right, shrink left while sum >= target, track min length.
relevant_options: Binary search on length with prefix sums
Prefix sums and binary search per start

questions.title: Number of Subarrays with Product Less Than K
questions.question: Count number of contiguous subarrays where product of elements < k.
questions.answer: Sliding window: expand right, shrink while product >=k, count += window size.
relevant_options: Binary search on length with product logs

questions.title: Shortest Subarray with Sum at Least K
questions.question: Find shortest subarray with sum >= k (array may have negatives).
questions.answer: Prefix sums with monotonic deque storing increasing prefix sums.

questions.title: Sort Array By Parity
questions.question: Sort array so all even numbers come before odd numbers (order within groups doesn't matter).
questions.answer: Two pointers: left for even, right for odd; swap when left odd and right even.
relevant_options: Create new array with two passes

questions.title: Sort Array By Parity II
questions.question: Sort array so even indices have even numbers, odd indices have odd numbers.
questions.answer: Two pointers: even pointer at 0, odd pointer at 1; swap when mismatch found.
relevant_options: Create new array and place even/odd

questions.title: Array Partition I
questions.question: Pair numbers to maximize sum of min of each pair.
questions.answer: Sort array, sum of elements at even indices (0,2,4,...).
relevant_options: Greedy pair smallest with next smallest
Sort then take every other element

questions.title: Largest Perimeter Triangle
questions.question: Given array of side lengths, find largest perimeter triangle (non-degenerate).
questions.answer: Sort descending; first triple where a < b + c gives max perimeter.

questions.title: Valid Triangle Number
questions.question: Count number of triplets that can form non-degenerate triangles.
questions.answer: Sort array, fix largest side c, use two pointers for a and b on left side.

questions.title: Three Sum Smaller
questions.question: Count triplets where sum < target.
questions.answer: Sort, fix first element, two pointers for remaining; if sum < target, count += right-left else right--.

questions.title: Number of Subsequences That Satisfy the Given Sum Condition
questions.question: Count number of subsequences (not necessarily contiguous) where min + max <= target.
questions.answer: Sort array; two pointers; for each left, find right such that nums[left]+nums[right]<=target; count += 2^(right-left).

questions.title: Most Profit Assigning Work
questions.question: Assign each worker a job (difficulty <= worker ability) to maximize total profit. Same job can be used multiple times.
questions.answer: Sort jobs by difficulty, compute max profit up to each difficulty; for each worker binary search best job.

questions.title: Advantage Shuffle
questions.question: Permute nums1 to maximize number of indices where nums1[i] > nums2[i] (advantage).
questions.answer: Sort both arrays; use two pointers: if smallest of nums1 > smallest of nums2, match; else match smallest to largest of nums2 (greedy).
relevant_options: Max heap for nums1 and assign

questions.title: Car Fleet
questions.question: Cars start at positions with speeds; determine number of car fleets reaching destination (cars that catch up become one fleet).
questions.answer: Sort by position descending; compute time to target; merge if time <= last time (faster behind catches up).

questions.title: Car Fleet II
questions.question: Return times when each car collides with next fleet, or -1 if never.
questions.answer: Monotonic decreasing stack of (speed, time); compute collision time with next fleet.

questions.title: Minimum Deletions to Make Array Divisible
questions.question: Remove smallest possible elements from nums so that LCM of remaining divides the product of second array?
questions.answer: Compute GCD of second array; find GCD of nums after sorting? Actually find min removals so that remaining LCM divides target.
relevant_options: Greedy remove smallest until condition satisfied

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Make at Most K Elements Equal
questions.question: Make k elements equal with cost = sum of increments. Find min cost.
questions.answer: Sort array, sliding window of size k, cost = k*median - sum(window) after adjusting.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements II
questions.question: Min moves to make all elements equal using +/-1; moves = sum of absolute differences to median.
questions.answer: Sorted array median minimizes sum of absolute deviations.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements
questions.question: Increment n-1 elements to make all equal. Min moves = sum - n*min.
questions.answer: Equivalent to decrementing one element each move, so moves = sum of (nums[i] - min).

questions.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
questions.question: Given array of n numbers in [1,n], find missing numbers (O(1) space).
questions.answer: Mark by making nums[abs(nums[i])-1] negative; positives indicate missing indices.
relevant_options: Use set and compare O(n) space
Sort then scan O(n log n)
XOR with indices (fails for multiple missing)

questions.title: Find the Celebrity
questions.question: Celebrity knows nobody and everyone knows celebrity. Find celebrity using O(n) calls to knows(a,b).
questions.answer: Find candidate by scanning: if candidate knows i, candidate = i; then verify candidate with all.

questions.title: First Bad Version
questions.question: Find first bad version in n versions using isBadVersion(version) API.
questions.answer: Binary search: if mid is bad, hi=mid else lo=mid+1.

questions.title: Guess Number Higher or Lower
questions.question: Guess number from 1 to n with guess(int) API (returns -1,0,1).
questions.answer: Binary search; adjust low/high based on return value.

questions.title: Pow(x, n)
questions.question: Implement pow(x, n) with O(log n) time.
questions.answer: Fast exponentiation: binary exponent recursion; handle negative n as 1/x^n.

questions.title: Sqrt(x)
questions.question: Return integer square root of x (floor).
questions.answer: Binary search between 0 and x; check mid*mid <= x.

questions.title: Valid Perfect Square
questions.question: Determine if integer is perfect square without using sqrt.
questions.answer: Binary search or Newton's method for exact square root.

questions.title: Ugly Number II
questions.question: Find nth ugly number (divisible by 2,3,5 only).
questions.answer: DP with three pointers: next ugly = min(2*dp[p2], 3*dp[p3], 5*dp[p5]).

questions.title: Happy Number
questions.question: Determine if number is happy (sum squares eventually 1).
questions.answer: Floyd cycle detection: slow and fast pointer; cycle detected if slow==fast and not 1.

questions.title: Excel Sheet Column Title
questions.question: Convert integer (1-based) to Excel column title (A, B, ..., Z, AA, AB).
questions.answer: While n>0: n--, char = 'A' + n%26, n /= 26.

questions.title: Excel Sheet Column Number
questions.question: Convert Excel column title to number (1-indexed).
questions.answer: Iterate: result = result*26 + (c - 'A' + 1).

questions.title: Factorial Trailing Zeroes
questions.question: Count number of trailing zeroes in n!.
questions.answer: Count factors of 5: sum floor(n/5) + floor(n/25) + floor(n/125) + ...

questions.title: Count Primes
questions.question: Count number of primes less than n.
questions.answer: Sieve of Eratosthenes: boolean array, mark multiples starting from p².

questions.title: Maximum Swap
questions.question: Swap two digits once to get maximum number.
questions.answer: Find largest digit from right that can be swapped with smaller digit on left; or use bucket of last occurrence.
relevant_options: Greedy from left: find max digit to right
Convert to array and sort for reference

questions.title: Repeated String Match
questions.question: Find minimum times to repeat string a so that b is substring of repeated a.
questions.answer: Repeat until length at least len(b); check substring; if not, repeat one more time.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Swaps to Make the String Balanced
questions.question: String has equal number of '[' and ']', swap any two chars. Min swaps to balance.
questions.answer: Track imbalance, answer = (max_imbalance+1)/2.

questions.title: Longest Word in Dictionary through Deleting
questions.question: Given string s and dictionary, find longest word that is subsequence of s (lex smallest if tie).
questions.answer: Sort words by length desc then lex; check subsequence using two pointers.

questions.title: Decode String
questions.question: Decode encoded string: k[encoded_string] pattern, e.g., '3[a]2[bc]' -> 'aaabcbc'.
questions.answer: Two stacks: one for numbers, one for strings; when ']', pop number and build repeated string.

questions.title: Remove K Digits
questions.question: Remove k digits to make smallest possible number (no leading zeros).
questions.answer: Monotonic increasing stack; pop when stack top > current and k>0; remove leading zeros.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops
questions.question: Car with target fuel starts with startFuel; stations at positions with fuel. Minimum stops to reach target.
questions.answer: Max-heap of fuel from passed stations; always go farthest possible, refuel from heap when needed.

questions.title: Reorganize String
questions.question: Rearrange string so no two adjacent characters are same. Return possible result or empty.
questions.answer: Max-heap by frequency; pop top two, append both, decrement counts, push back if still >0.

questions.title: Partition Labels
questions.question: Partition string into as many parts as possible so each character appears in at most one part.
questions.answer: Store last occurrence of each char, then expand segment until current index equals last occurrence of any char in segment.

questions.title: Candy Crush (1D)
questions.question: Remove three or more consecutive identical characters repeatedly (like Candy Crush).
questions.answer: Use two pointers: write pointer, count consecutive; if count >=3, skip; else write them.

questions.title: String Compression
questions.question: Compress char array by replacing consecutive duplicates with char and count (if >1).
questions.answer: Two pointers: read pointer, write pointer; count consecutive, write char and if count>1 write digits.
relevant_options: Create new string then copy back

questions.title: Decode the Slanted Ciphertext
questions.question: Decode string encoded in slanted pattern (like reading diagonals).
questions.answer: Place string in matrix with given rows, read column by column top to bottom, skip trailing spaces.

questions.title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
questions.question: Find maximum XOR of any two numbers in array.
questions.answer: Use Trie (bitwise) or greedy: for each prefix, check if complement exists.

questions.title: Single Number II
questions.question: Every element appears three times except one. Find the single one.
questions.answer: Use bitwise: ones, twos variables; ones = (ones ^ num) & ~twos; twos = (twos ^ num) & ~ones.
relevant_options: Sort and scan O(n log n)

questions.title: Single Number III
questions.question: Two numbers appear once, others twice. Find both numbers.
questions.answer: XOR all -> xorResult; find rightmost set bit in xorResult; partition numbers into two groups based on that bit.

questions.title: Sum of Two Integers
questions.question: Calculate sum of two integers without using + or -.
questions.answer: Use XOR for sum, AND for carry; carry <<=1; repeat until carry==0.
relevant_options: Use logarithm and exponent (fails for negative)

questions.title: Number of 1 Bits
questions.question: Count number of 1 bits (Hamming weight) of unsigned integer.
questions.answer: n & (n-1) clears lowest set bit; count iterations.

questions.title: Reverse Bits
questions.question: Reverse bits of 32-bit unsigned integer.
questions.answer: Mask and swap approach: swap neighboring bits, then 2-bit groups, 4-bit groups, etc.
relevant_options: Loop 32 times shifting result left and adding LSB

questions.title: Hamming Distance
questions.question: Hamming distance between two integers (number of differing bits).
questions.answer: XOR then count bits (popcount).

questions.title: Total Hamming Distance
questions.question: Sum of Hamming distances for all pairs in array.
questions.answer: For each bit position, count ones and zeros; contribution = ones * zeros.
relevant_options: XOR all pairs then sum popcount

questions.title: Power of Four
questions.question: Return true if n is power of four.
questions.answer: n>0, (n & (n-1))==0 (power of two) and n & 0x55555555 !=0 (bits in odd positions).

questions.title: Find the Difference
questions.question: String t is string s with one random letter added. Find the added letter.
questions.answer: XOR all characters of s and t, result is added letter.

questions.title: Counting Bits
questions.question: Return array count of 1 bits for numbers from 0 to n.

questions.title: Gray Code
questions.question: Generate Gray code sequence for n bits (consecutive differ by one bit).
questions.answer: Sequence = i xor (i>>1) for i from 0 to 2^n -1.

questions.title: IP to CIDR
questions.question: Given start IP and number n, represent range as minimal list of CIDR blocks.
questions.answer: Convert to uint, while n>0, find largest block size, add CIDR, subtract size.

questions.title: Design Hit Counter
questions.question: Count hits in last 5 minutes (300 seconds).
questions.answer: Use queue of timestamps; hit: add timestamp; getHits: remove older than 300s, return queue size.
relevant_options: Binary search on sorted timestamp list

questions.title: Logger Rate Limiter
questions.question: Logger prints message only if it hasn't been printed in last 10 seconds.
questions.answer: HashMap storing last printed timestamp; if current >= last+10, print and update.

questions.title: Rate Limiter
questions.question: Design rate limiter that allows max N requests per minute per user.
questions.answer: Token bucket or sliding window log: store timestamps per user in queue, remove older than window.

questions.title: Moving Average from Data Stream
questions.question: Calculate moving average of last size elements.
questions.answer: Queue with sum variable; add new, remove old if size exceeds, update sum.
relevant_options: Store array and compute each time

questions.title: First Unique Number
questions.question: Track stream of numbers; support add and showFirstUnique.
questions.answer: Hash map for count, queue for order; lazy delete from queue front when count>1.

questions.title: Underground System
questions.question: Design system to track check-in/check-out times and calculate average travel time between stations.
questions.answer: Two maps: checkIns (id->(station,time)), and averages (startStation+endStation -> (sum, count)).
relevant_options: Store all trips and compute on query

questions.title: Design Twitter
questions.question: Design simplified Twitter with postTweet, getNewsFeed, follow, unfollow.
questions.answer: User has tweet list and follow set; feed uses heap of tweets from followed users (latest 10).
relevant_options: Pull every time from all followed

questions.title: Design a Stack With Increment Operation
questions.question: Stack with push, pop, and inc(k,val) that adds val to bottom k elements.
questions.answer: Use lazy increment array: store increment for index; when pop, add pending increment to value.
relevant_options: Actually increment top k each time (O(k))

questions.title: Car Pooling
questions.question: Given trips [numPassengers, from, to] and capacity, determine if possible to pick up/drop off all.
questions.answer: Difference array: for each trip, add passengers at from, subtract at to; check cumulative at any point <= capacity.

questions.title: Meeting Rooms II
questions.question: Find minimum number of meeting rooms required for given intervals.
questions.answer: Sort starts and ends separately, two pointers: if start < end, need room else free room.
relevant_options: Sort by start then assign room

questions.title: Employee Free Time
questions.question: Given each employee's schedule (sorted intervals), find all common free time intervals.
questions.answer: Merge all intervals, then gaps between merged intervals are free times.
relevant_options: Use heap to merge k sorted lists

questions.title: Non-overlapping Intervals
questions.question: Find minimum number of intervals to remove to make the rest non-overlapping.
questions.answer: Sort by end time; greedy: keep intervals that end earliest, remove overlapping.
relevant_options: Sort by start and use heap

questions.title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
questions.question: Find minimum arrows to burst all balloons (interval overlap).
questions.answer: Sort by end, shoot arrow at end, burst all intervals containing that point.

questions.title: Insert Interval
questions.question: Insert new interval into sorted non-overlapping intervals, merge if needed.
questions.answer: Add all intervals before new, merge overlapping, add rest.

questions.title: Interval List Intersections
questions.question: Find intersections of two lists of intervals.
questions.answer: Two pointers: start = max(a.start, b.start), end = min(a.end, b.end); if start<=end, add. Move pointer with smaller end.
relevant_options: Merge both lists then find overlaps
Use priority queue for min start
Binary search in one list for intervals

questions.title: Data Stream as Disjoint Intervals
questions.question: Add numbers from stream, return list of disjoint intervals.
questions.answer: Use TreeMap of intervals; add number: merge with left/right if contiguous.
relevant_options: Sorted list of numbers then build intervals on query

questions.title: Summary Ranges
questions.question: Given sorted unique array, return summary ranges (e.g., [0,1,2,4,5,7] -> ['0->2','4->5','7']).
questions.answer: Two pointers: start of range, extend while consecutive, add range when break.
relevant_options: Create list of pairs then format

questions.title: Missing Ranges
questions.question: Find missing ranges in sorted array from lower to upper inclusive.
questions.answer: Check between lower and first element, then between consecutive, then last and upper.

questions.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
questions.question: Find maximum average of subarray of length k.
questions.answer: Sliding window sum, compute average, track max.
relevant_options: Prefix sums then compute each window O(n)

questions.title: Maximum Average Subarray II
questions.question: Find maximum average of subarray with length at least k.
questions.answer: Binary search on average; check if there exists subarray >=k with average >=mid using prefix sums transformed.
relevant_options: Brute force O(n²) for small n

questions.title: Most Frequent Subtree Sum
questions.question: Find most frequent sum of any subtree (including leaf only).
questions.answer: Post-order DFS: compute sum for node, update frequency map, track max frequency.
relevant_options: Use map of sum to count

questions.title: Subtree of Another Tree
questions.question: Check if tree t is subtree of tree s (exact structure).
questions.answer: Serialize both trees with null markers; check if t's serialization appears in s's serialization.
relevant_options: Recursive: check if t equals s or t in left/right
Two DFS: find matching root then compare

questions.title: Delete Nodes and Return Forest
questions.question: Delete given nodes from binary tree; return forest of remaining roots.
questions.answer: Post-order DFS: if node to delete, add children as new roots, return null to parent; else return node.

questions.title: Distribute Coins in Binary Tree
questions.question: Given binary tree with coins on nodes (total coins = nodes), move coins adjacent to balance. Min moves = sum of absolute excess.
questions.answer: DFS returns net coins needed (excess/deficit); move count += abs(dfs returned).
relevant_options: Compute prefix sums of subtree coin count

questions.title: Kth Smallest Number in Multiplication Table
questions.question: Find kth smallest number in m x n multiplication table (table[i][j] = i*j).
questions.answer: Binary search on value; count how many <= mid using floor(mid/i) summing to m*n

questions.title: Kth Smallest Element in a Sorted Matrix
questions.question: Find kth smallest element in matrix where rows and columns are sorted.
questions.answer: Binary search on value, count elements <= mid using O(n) counting: start from bottom-left.
relevant_options: Min-heap merging rows O(k log n)
Flatten and sort O(n² log n)

questions.title: Find K Pairs with Smallest Sums
questions.question: Given two sorted arrays, find k pairs with smallest sums (u from nums1, v from nums2).
questions.answer: Min-heap with (sum, i, j); push (nums1[0]+nums2[0],0,0); push (i+1,j) and (i,j+1) with visited set.

questions.title: Find K-th Smallest Pair Distance
questions.question: Find kth smallest distance between any two numbers in array.
questions.answer: Sort array; binary search on distance; count pairs with distance <= mid using two pointers.
relevant_options: Generate all pairs O(n² log n)

questions.title: Maximum Swap
questions.question: Swap two digits once to get maximum number.
questions.answer: Find largest digit from right that can be swapped with smaller digit on left; or use bucket of last occurrence.
relevant_options: Greedy from left: find max digit to right
Convert to array and sort for reference

questions.title: Number of Sets of K Non-Overlapping Line Segments
questions.question: Count ways to place k non-overlapping segments on a line of n points.
questions.answer: DP or combinatorics: ways = C(n+k-1, 2k) or using stars and bars.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Move All Balls to Each Box
questions.question: Given binary string (0=empty,1=ball), compute moves needed to move all balls to each box (adjacent move cost 1).
questions.answer: Two passes: left to right cumulate moves, right to left cumulate moves.
relevant_options: Prefix sums of counts and distances

questions.title: Minimum Distance to the Target Element
questions.question: Find minimum distance from target element to any occurrence of target in array (including itself).
questions.answer: Scan left to right, track last occurrence; answer = min(current - last) or just 0 if equal?
relevant_options: Store all indices then compute differences

questions.title: Maximum Element After Decreasing and Rearranging
questions.question: Rearrange array such that arr[0]=1, adjacent diff <=1, each <= original. Maximize last element.
questions.answer: Sort, set arr[0]=1, for i: arr[i] = min(arr[i], arr[i-1]+1).

questions.title: Minimum Time to Make Rope Colorful
questions.question: Remove balloons (letters) to avoid consecutive same colors, with removal costs. Min total cost.
questions.answer: Group consecutives, keep max cost in group, sum rest.
